narrative: OMX30 enters Monday supported by strong NASDAQ performance (+1.91%) and positive Shanghai (+1.77%), though Continental Europe shows caution (DAX -0.10%, CAC -0.41%). The index remains firmly bullish at +7.9% above its 200-MA and +1.6% above 50-MA, but technicals warn of overbought conditions: Stochastic K/D readings of 82.4/88.0 suggest mean reversion risk in the near term. Yields have fallen 27bp on the US 10Y (4.451%), which supports tech valuations but slightly compresses bank NIM expansion. VIX spike to 17.25 (+5.18%) and UK political uncertainty (Starmer resignation) inject caution into the session open.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens +0.4% to +0.7% on positive overnight Asia/NASDAQ signals, but watch for profit-taking given Stochastic overbought extremes. Key support sits at 3085 (lower Bollinger band); resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson) benefit from USD/SEK +0.20%, but sentiment remains fragile with CAC weakness and VIX spike. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken, Swedbank) face headwinds from falling yields—NIM compression outweighs any positive beta. Boliden outperforms on copper firmness (+0.11%). Watch for mean reversion into midday; ADX at 18.6 is at inflection point for trend strength confirmation. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is key catalyst for afternoon momentum.
drivers: OMX30 +7.9% above 200-MA in sustained bull regime
Stochastic overbought (K=82.4) signals near-term consolidation risk

sectorImpacts.reason: USD/SEK strength (+0.20%) favours exporter valuations; NASDAQ +1.91% supports sentiment for industrial cyclicals.
US 10Y yield fell 27bp to 4.451%; NIM compression risk outweighs beta support. Defensive rotation limits upside.
Copper +0.11% and gold +0.06% drive direct revenue uplift; Boliden a strong long candidate.
NASDAQ +1.91% provides positive sentiment offset; falling yields support DCF, but Stochastic overbought limits new long initiation.
VIX spike (+5.18%) triggers modest risk-off rotation, but not yet sufficient to drive strong defensive inflow.
Falling yields (US 10Y -27bp) compress real estate DCF valuations; refinancing risk remains structural headwind.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.11% and gold +0.06% both support; structural commodity tailwind into H2 2026.
USD/SEK strength (+0.20%) plus NASDAQ confidence supports industrial export narrative.
NASDAQ +1.91% overnight is positive, but Stochastic overbought and VIX spike counsel caution on entry timing.
watchList.catalyst: 5G capex data; mean reversion on Stochastic

watchList.reason: Falling US yields (-27bp) compress NIM expectations; no catalyst for reversal in near term.
Strong USD/SEK and NASDAQ positive sentiment support auto cyclical; watch for profit-taking on Stochastic extremes.

keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (implied Monday open level; yesterday close reference)

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic data today. US Jobless Claims (initial + continuing) at 14:30 CET—watch for labour market softening signals. UK political developments may drive sterling/EUR moves.
topRisk: Stochastic overbought (K=82.4, D=88.0) signals imminent mean reversion; VIX spike to 17.25 (+5.18%) may accelerate profit-taking into midday. UK PM resignation (Starmer) adds political uncertainty; watch for contagion to SEK via risk-off flows. Falling yields compress tech/real estate valuations despite overnight NASDAQ strength.
technicalSummary: MACD above signal line (histogram +5.224) = bullish structure
RSI-14 at 60.91 = neutral (not yet overbought), but Stochastic K/D at 82.4/88.0 = overbought short-term
ADX 18.6 = moderate trend strength; bullish DI bias intact but near confirmation inflection
Bollinger %B=0.70 in upper half = stretched but not extreme; width 4.77% = volatility manageable
52-week positioning at 92% of range, -2.0% from all-time high = profit-taking zone
